title: Day 4/10 of useless college facts and stats
body:
#### COLLEGE PRESIDENTS WHO MADE BANK

* Former Bryant University **President Ronald K. Machtley** made **$6,283,616** in 2017. He stepped down in June 2020.
* Johnson and Wales University's former **Chancellor and President John J. Bowen** made **$5,363,616** in 2017. He retired in 2018.
* Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute's **President Shirley Ann Jackson** made **$5,155,038** in 2017.
* The University of Pennsylvania's **President Amy Gutmann** made **$2,930,315** in 2017.
* Georgia State University's **President Mark P. Becker** made **$2,806,517** in 2019.

#### DEGREES WITH WORST RETURN ON INVESTMENT

* Sociology
* Fine arts
* Education
* Theology
* Hospitality/Tourism
* Nutrition
* Psychology
* Communications

#### COLLEGES WITH WORST ROI

3. Maine College of Art — Portland, Maine  
ROI: -$163,6002. 

2. Miles College — Fairfield, Alabama  
ROI: -$164,6001. 

1. Mississippi Valley State University — Itta Bena, Mississippi  
ROI: -$174,800

#### COLLEGES WITH BEST ROI (2016 DATA)

3 (tie). **SUNY-MARITIME COLLEGE (in state)**  
20-Year Net ROI: $945,000  
4-Year Cost: $89,000  
Average Loan Amount: $32,000

3 (tie). **HARVEY MUDD COLLEGE**  
20-Year Net ROI: $945,000  
4-Year Cost: $249,000  
Average Loan Amount: $24,4002. 

**MASSACHUSETTS INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY**  
20-Year Net ROI: $972,000  
4-Year Cost: $232,000  
Average Loan Amount: $32,2001. 

**CALIFORNIA INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY**  
20-Year Net ROI: $973,000  
4-Year Cost: $230,000  
Average Loan Amount: $19,000
author: DavidTej

top_comments.text: No surprise MIT and Caltech have one of the best ROIs. Thanks for sharing!
top_comments.author: [deleted]

top_comments.text: Damn I don’t live far from SUNY maritime, who knew
top_comments.author: 8bitzawad

title: Is Harvard really the golden ticket for poor students?
body:
It doesn’t appear to be from these data.

“The median family income of a student from Harvard is $168,800, and 67% come from the top 20 percent. About 1.8% of students at Harvard came from a poor family but became a rich adult.”

By comparison, Mississippi Valley State is 3.9%.

https://www.nytimes.com/interactive/projects/college-mobility/harvard-university
author: wrroyals

top_comments.text: That's because not many poor students get in. However, the ones who do mostly become wealthier adults
top_comments.author: AllUsernamesTaken711

top_comments.text: I don’t think you know what a “golden ticket” is. Yes it’s very rare for a poor student to get into Harvard, but those who do are able to achieve massive economic growth.
top_comments.author: namey-name-name

top_comments.text:
58% chance to move from bottom fifth income to top fifth income. Seems great to me. That looks like a golden ticket to me. 

Also, the major your major in + the work you put in matters more. A school name will (generally) only get you so far. 

And from my personal experience, those who come from more affluent parents tend to be better educated, etc. (due to the advantages). Those students tend to do well in college which might make those without strong academic foundations to lose courage/motivation. 

Anyways, the biggest issue is upbringing when it comes to choosing majors. Upper middle income class kids are more likely to want to become doctors, lawyers, engineers, etc. while lower income class kids are more likely to want to become teachers, social workers, etc. 

I also noted from my own experience (anecdotes here, not statistics) that many from the lower income families tended to major in fields like social sciences, zoology, African American studies, etc. and did not pursue grad school. Friends I know from upper middle class almost all unanimously went for fields like biomedical engineering, chemical engineering, computer science, pre-med, physics, etc. In fact it was quite funny how one of my friends tried to "help" during first year of college by trying to cement the idea of "you need money to survive. You need to change majors to something more practical." The peers from lower income families didn't seem to care about money as much as they were more focused on "helping the poor" (which I found ironic in some sense as the best way to help the poor is to get your family out of the lower income class first).

Major/degree has more impact than anything else and there tends to be a difference in the preference of certain fields by different ethnic groups and social classes (again all just results of upbringings).

Anyways to your question? Yes. 58% chance to move to top fifth income from bottom fifth? Heck yes.

Also, wtf is "rich". What's the measurement.
top_comments.author: Fwellimort

top_comments.text:
This is not “percentage **of poor students** who gained…”, it‘s “percentage **of all students** who gained”.

The family income numbers clearly show Harvard has many fewer “poor”students, so a much lower percentage is even possible.

I’m guessing “percentage of students with under 1000 SAT scores that are employed at graduation” is also much higher at MVS. Because Harvard would be 0% as it has no students entering with SAT scores under 1000. Does 0% mean it’s a complete failure at students getting jobs?

&#x200B;

The median income of a MVS graduate at age 34 is $24,900.

The median income of a Harvard graduate at age 34 is $81,500.

&#x200B;

Let’s actually compare similar populations:

The average income percentile **of a poor student** graduating from Harvard is 71st.

The average income percentile **of a poor student** graduating from MVS is 44th.

&#x200B;

The income numbers indicate the average poor student is much more successful (measured by income, as you seem to define it) graduating from Harvard.

So a poor person admitted to Harvard is indeed  much more likely to be successful than a poor person admitted to MVS.
top_comments.author: RichInPitt

top_comments.text: Hell yes. Any of the schools that promise to meet 100% need without loans are a golden ticket for poor students.
top_comments.author: Dragonflies3

top_comments.text: Beats Princeton at 1.3%, but falls short of Yale at 2.1%.
top_comments.author: wrroyals

top_comments.text:
When will people on this sub realize that your salary is not wholly determined by the university you went to, and that university graduates often trade off earning potential for other lifestyle preferences?

Harvard admits fewer poor students than Mississippi Valley State does, I'm sure, but in addition to that, it's likely that a higher proportion of the poor students who go to Harvard choose to major (or concentrate, whatever) in subjects like folklore & mythology, Africana studies, theater, comparative literature, religion, etc. and go on to work in academia, the non-profit sector, and the arts. So they're less likely to be rich.

That doesn't mean their education failed to uplift them; on the contrary, it gave them access to realms of human knowledge they couldn't have afforded to explore at most other institutions and gave them the sense of security (in terms of generous financial aid and the power of the Harvard name) to pursue their true passions and interests in and beyond university. If they're satisfied with their career choices, that's all that matters.
top_comments.author: tractata

top_comments.text: There been some interesting studies. Many kids coming from lower income households major in things like African-American studies or sociology or education, which are all low paying majors compared to something that gets you to finance/tech which is considerably higher paying. So lots of confounding factors.
top_comments.author: InnerBeauty1

title: Touring Mississippi Valley State University!!
body: Sorry to say but as the 1st poster said below this needs some real beautification. Some restoration, revitalization, redo is in order. Students already know this I'm sure but ya'll need to push for some kind of state funding if ya'll haven't already.  Just speaking from a place of care and wanting to see hbcu schools thrive

title: Touring Mississippi Valley State University!!
body: I got a question so i recently applied and during the application process they asked me for my shot records is that normal?

title: Welcome to my ALMA MATER: MISSISSIPPI VALLEY STATE UNIVERSITY!!
body: Thanks for the video.  It truly brings back memories.  I am from North Carolina c/o1983.  Valley is a wonderful experience.  The first few weeks were rough adjusting to being so far from home.  It still feels like home when I return.  There are no professors on any campus like those at Valley.
author: @carlaevans6833

title: Welcome to my ALMA MATER: MISSISSIPPI VALLEY STATE UNIVERSITY!!
body: My brother attended MVSU and played football there - I believe back in the 70's. I had the pleasure of visiting this campus and it stands out as a most memorable one ....I'll be sure to forward this video to him. Great stuff!
author: @vfiel4111

title: The JSU Sports Network Reviews Jackson State’s Win Over MS Valley State
body: The reason  JSU is not dominant is because of favoritism. The first game we were dominating because we got everyone involved. Now the announcer is continuing to call the same names.
author: @yocreoibelieve1068

body:
Juddeye1 observations of problems

#2) Shame on deion ...shadeur played one to many series in 4th quarter...risking injury
#3) the JSU kicker still has not kick field goal over 40 yards...what is deion afraid of (he has passed on 3 opportunities )

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I was in psych lock down 2 weeks ago:  no phone or computer, no fresh air, no sunshine, no therapy offered, I felt like I came out worse than I went in, and I was suicidal.  Things MUST change.  Our society is failing us.
author: @zengwenyoga2894

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: To Rob…I remember first hand the feeling of visiting my wife in the psych ward and then going home to two young kids. In a couple of weeks, we celebrate 48 years. Hang tough, man. She’s an impressive lady.
author: @rcp2212

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I was held for 5 days involuntarily after visiting the ER for panic attacks and horrible anxiety I've battled my whole life. Never once saw a doctor and never recieved a single medication including the ones I was prescribed. No clothing, no phones, no outdoor time, no visitors, and the worst part was I just had oral surgery and had lost all my teeth due to a bad infection in my jaw bone which needed a bone graft so I was unable to eat because they had no accommodation for that diet. Let's just say that's a birthday I will never forget. When asking for meds, the nurse pulled my file and there was an intake sheet with a picture of a 14 year old female in it ( I am a 32 year old male). They couldnt even handle the correct documents to assist in any sort of help.  Still to this day I have ptsd due to my stay.
author: @TheOneTheOnlyKJ

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: It is shocking how different psych wards around the world are. I'm in Germany and here you are not supposed to be all alone in your room. You get a routine to follow. And this routine contains the gym, group therapy, single therapy, arts, meditation and other activities. There is a lot of talking between patients and the staff. Its like a controlled holiday.
author: @larakorrat

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I've been in a psych ward over ten times during my 39 years of life. I'm haven't been in a psych ward since April of 2007. Which is awesome and I'm so proud of myself for that.
author: @Bubbles1984

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: How difficult and painful it is to live with mental disorders. Those who do not have them will never understand what hell it is.
author: @purpur7187

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: You're very brave to put yourself out there when you're in such a vulnerable position. Be proud of that, not many people could do it.
author: @ronr6450

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: She's so full of life and very articulate. Schizophrenia is such a cruel disease. I wish her nothing but the best.😊
author: @mike4619

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: This is destroying me! For the last 3 years my 62 year old husband of 35 years had a psychotic break. It reached a point where he is committed by the state. He was starving himself. I know he’s sitting there everyday, just sitting. Keep running little girl, keep fighting, the people who love you need you to fight.
author: @Radar2727

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I've been committed about 15 times. I've finally found a balance with therapy and medication. It's been hell, but I keep chugging along.
author: @mr.bipolar3344

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: Last week I went to the ER and someone was having a some sort of psychiatric break. She was clearly terrified and screaming. Seeing the way staff treated her was heartbreaking. I kept hearing her screaming “get off of me” and hearing the nurses giggling about “the pipes on that chick” and “it’s so and sos favorite patient”. It was very disheartening.
author: @meaganhiller3029

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I have been admitted 3 times to a phych ward  some of the nicest most supportive people i ever met were patients
author: @MissMadiGD

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I'm an american, but I live in the UK. I was in the psyc ward for 3 months in full-blown psychosis. They were really good to me & the patients were great. I didn't eat for the first month but the food was actually really good. I was on a 5150 in my teens and the psyc ward in the states...it was the most lonely depressing time of my life. The conditions make a huge difference.
author: @GinaRossana-hl6ph

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: This was both hard to watch and incredible to watch.  My extreme admiration goes out to you for documenting something so difficult.  People casually talk of "mental health" as this amorphous concept.  Your bravery in putting yourself out there and attaching a face to a facet of it is extraordinary.  There are so many different struggles people go through.  It's painful seeing someone having to live like that on a daily basis.  I hope you realize just how much of a good you're doing by showing everyone that you can put one foot in front of the other, and that there is no stigma, only a struggle that people have to face down regularly, which takes a lot of strength.  Thank you so much for what you show us all.
author: @revcliff

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: Im Nurse who worked on Psychic Ward for year s. You see so many cases from Drug Addiction, Mental illness so much more. God BLESS your Journey
author: @melanieeggers9667

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body:
this is a freaking 5 star hotel.....the place I went...you can not go outside.... cant have phones, cant talk to the other sex, also we could not even be alone any where....wow. I know this is a year old, but you are a strong person and a beautiful soul.

Edit* Thank you for all the likes.
author: @dalekkgames5430

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body:
I am in a YouTube rabbit hole right now. I do not experience anything remotely like what you are living with. I admire the hell out of how you have chosen to beautify and help our world. You are a strong and courageous woman.
Thank you.
author: @Razedot

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: It always shocks me how different psych wards can be in different places. I was admitted to a psychiatric institution involuntarily and was there for 2 weeks. I had a roommate, we weren't allowed outside of our room from 9pm-7am and weren't allowed back in our room from 7am-7pm. Eating breakfast, lunch, and dinner was mandatory and we all had to be present in the cafeteria, no one was allowed to stay back. We had to attend group sessions to talk about battling mental illness and coping mechanisms. It wasn't very effective. We had a TV in the common area, but they wouldn't turn it on unless it was to watch a movie. I spent all of my free time reading and coloring. We weren't allowed to go outside and only had one window in the whole ward. It literally felt like prison.
author: @halidyer5595

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: Spent 40+ years in and out of psychiatric hospitals until we discovered I had a biological problem, not a psychological one. Changed my diet and changed my life. Been mentally free for over 10 years now.
author: @66sprint

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body:
My mom was in psych wards multiple times. The last time, I warned the nurses that she was lying and just putting on a face of someone that was “fine” and was there for no reason. I told them to not listen to anything she was saying and the nurses said that they would tell the doctors. She was out and back home two day later.. Exactly ONE week later, she committed suicide!
My mom was always the person that everyone wanted to be around. She was beautiful, kind, intelligent, and the life of the party. NO ONE expected her to do what she did EXCEPT her kids. My brothers and I seen this side of her every day. It was very hard listening to the side that everyone else got because she had been saying for five years that this was what she was going to do😢
Miss you mom❤
author: @squirrelsquad3.0

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: As a teen I was placed in an involuntary hold, there was some effed up things that I personally recall and witnessed that I was too young to morally realize. It was a traumatic experience in itself but actually ended up having some very real and genuine conversations with some of the other patients. Hope they’re all doing well today.
author: @Lovuii

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: Sleep is so important for any patient.   We've known that forever. Sadly, hospitals in the U.S.  haven't figured that out.  You are very articulate.  Unfortunately, from what I have heard, there is far from enough activities for psychiatric patients.  Patients sitting around is  typical for psychiatric patients.  .
author: @monaoconnell5650

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I was in the psych ward after trying to unsubscribe from life. It help somewhat but they also told me my current doctor was prescribing meds that interact and way too high a dose… sending you ❤
author: @kristenkirk6108

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: As a schizotypal person, I find this video instructive.  I have similar delusions to Lauren, but somewhat differently.  Lauren is on the cutting edge of mental health for us neurodivergent folks.  I applaud her bravery!
author: @ralphloen6901

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body:
I was in the psych ward a few years ago. All I can say is that the patients helped and supported me way more than the drs.
Yes it is absolutely boring, Especially when you're in lock down as you're at risk of self harm.
But sometimes our minds need that quiet and stillness also, even if it feels forced.
I do agree however that we need to improve on mental health treatment 100%.
author: @natahliak7691

title: Miss Valley State Coach: Chicago Football Classic 'worst experience of my life'
body: So, are any of the problems in Chicago due to the AD? There shouldn't be a lot of changes and confusion with a game. There needs to be a discussion with the promoter and management team to make sure things run smoothly and the teams are comfortable.
author: @SonicBoomC98

title: Miss Valley State Coach: Chicago Football Classic 'worst experience of my life'
body: HBCUs need to stop playing in these classic. U lose a home game & the $$$$ ? I wouldn’t play in one of them unless ur paying a significant amount of $$$$ .
author: @thetruth1635

title: Miss Valley State Coach: Chicago Football Classic 'worst experience of my life'
body: I disagree with the notion that we shouldn't play in any Classics. The Classics are part of the reason for the popularity of HBCU football as well how we were able to recruit students from all over the country. Now should we be playing every Classic there is? No. At one point Classics were like Bowl games. There were just too many of them. There were too many random ones just thrown together.  There is nothing wrong with most of the ones the top brands or the top 6-8 Classics. Some Classics don't make sense because of the location or the teams involved,  but at the same time some.of these schools aren't exactly raking in the money at home. And fans need to stop lying. As much as we say we want more home games, we still circle the games in the popular black cities to attend and sometimes skip a home game or two.
author: @SonicBoomC98

title: Miss Valley State Coach: Chicago Football Classic 'worst experience of my life'
body: Sheesh…you lost to a bad Division II school in Central State.  Delta State about to tear Valley out the frame
author: @Truth1824

title: A COLLEGE DAY IN MY LIFE | JUNIOR YEAR | MISSISSIPPI STATE UNIVERSITY
body: Heard great things about this school. I hate when a bump just appears on my face too!😩 I love those boots. Great video, I enjoyed watching.
author: @LawrenceFamilyTv
